title: agrimonia striata subsp. pilosa
intro: Agrimonia striata subsp. pilosa, also known as soft-leaved agrimony, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the rosinweed family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributing to its medicinal properties, and has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, respiratory issues, and wounds. Herbal preparations of soft-leaved agrimony include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions, while also supporting immune function and promoting overall well-being.
